edges.description: Abnormal cartilage calcification and endochondral ossification produce the characteristic short tibiae, short metacarpals, and vertebral abnormalities.
edges.source: Abnormal Cartilage Calcification and Endochondral Ossification
edges.target: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: Abnormal cartilage calcification produces punctate epiphyseal calcifications.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: The limb skeletal pattern includes short central metacarpals.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: The dysplasia includes craniofacial skeletal involvement with flat midface and nose.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern
edges.target: Depressed Nasal Bridge and Flat Midface

edges.description: The named skeletal pattern includes short tibiae.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: Vertebral involvement in the skeletal pattern produces coronal cleft vertebrae.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: Disproportionate skeletal growth can produce persistent short stature.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: Skeletal dysplasia can complicate joint mobility and produce progressive limitations.
intermediate_mechanisms: Dysplastic short-limbed skeletal development with contracture or joint limitation.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: The lower-limb skeletal pattern predisposes to recurrent patellar instability.
intermediate_mechanisms: Lower-limb dysplasia and altered patellofemoral alignment.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: Vertebral-body involvement in CDP-TM produces platyspondyly.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: The long-bone skeletal pattern includes bowed humeri.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: The skeletal dysplasia can involve the hips and require orthopedic intervention.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: Chronic serous otitis media is observed in CDP-TM, but the specific intermediate mechanism is not resolved.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

edges.description: Vertebral skeletal involvement can progress to spinal canal stenosis.
intermediate_mechanisms: Vertebral skeletal dysplasia and spinal canal narrowing.
edges.source: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

nodes.description: CDP-TM affects growth plate and epiphyseal cartilage, producing punctate calcifications, vertebral clefts, and later abnormal metaphyseal or epiphyseal morphology as calcifications remodel.
nodes.id: Abnormal Cartilage Calcification and Endochondral Ossification

biological_processes: cartilage development involved in endochondral bone morphogenesis

evidence.snippet: Consistent radiologic manifestations in the newborn infant are discrete calcific stippling, coronal clefts of vertebral bodies, short tibiae, and shortness of the 2nd and 3rd metacarpal bones.

nodes.description: The humeri can be markedly short and bowed in CDP-TM, as seen on prenatal CT and postnatal radiographs.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of chondrodysplasia punctata tibia-metacarpal type using multidetector CT and three-dimensional reconstruction.
evidence.snippet: the tibiae were much shorter than the fibulae, the humeri were very short and bowed, and severe platyspondyly was evident.

nodes.description: Chronic serous otitis media requiring surgical drainage tubes has been observed in CDP-TM during childhood.

evidence.reference_title: Longterm follow-up in chondrodysplasia punctata, tibia-metacarpal type, demonstrating natural history.
evidence.snippet: Two patients had chronic serous otitis media requiring tympanostomy tubes during childhood.

nodes.description: Vertebral coronal clefts are part of the newborn radiographic pattern.

evidence.snippet: Consistent radiologic manifestations in the newborn infant are discrete calcific stippling, coronal clefts of vertebral bodies, short tibiae, and shortness of the 2nd and 3rd metacarpal bones.

nodes.description: Flat midface and nose are principal clinical manifestations of the disorder.
nodes.id: Depressed Nasal Bridge and Flat Midface

evidence.snippet: Its principal clinical manifestations are flat midface and nose, short limbs, and otherwise normal development.

nodes.description: The disorder produces short tibiae, short second to fourth metacarpals, vertebral clefts, and variable later orthopedic complications.
nodes.id: Disproportionate Limb and Vertebral Skeletal Pattern

evidence.snippet: Radiologic findings in the older child include shortness of tibiae and the 3rd and 4th metacarpals.

nodes.description: Punctate epiphyseal calcifications are the hallmark early radiologic finding.

evidence.snippet: Consistent radiologic manifestations in the newborn infant are discrete calcific stippling, coronal clefts of vertebral bodies, short tibiae, and shortness of the 2nd and 3rd metacarpal bones.

nodes.description: Hip dysplasia requiring orthopedic intervention is a recognized long-term complication of CDP-TM.

evidence.reference_title: Longterm follow-up in chondrodysplasia punctata, tibia-metacarpal type, demonstrating natural history.
evidence.snippet: One patient had hip dysplasia requiring orthopedic surgical intervention. All patients had recurrent patella dislocation.

nodes.description: Joint contractures or progressive limitations can complicate the skeletal dysplasia.

evidence.reference_title: Progressive joint limitations as the first alarming signs in a boy with short - limbed dwarfism: A case report.
evidence.snippet: CASE PRESENTATION: We report on a 5-year-old boy of Austrian origin who manifests progressive joint limitations in connection with a dysplastic form of short-limbed dwarfism namely chondrodysplasia punctata-tibial-metacarpal-type.

nodes.description: Management is supportive and complication-directed, including monitoring for spinal stenosis, patellar dislocation, hip dysplasia, and other orthopedic complications.

evidence.reference_title: Longterm follow-up in chondrodysplasia punctata, tibia-metacarpal type, demonstrating natural history.
evidence.snippet: One patient had hip dysplasia requiring orthopedic surgical intervention. All patients had recurrent patella dislocation.

nodes.description: Flattened vertebral bodies (platyspondyly) are observed on neonatal and prenatal imaging and may partially resolve with growth.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of chondrodysplasia punctata tibia-metacarpal type using multidetector CT and three-dimensional reconstruction.
evidence.snippet: the tibiae were much shorter than the fibulae, the humeri were very short and bowed, and severe platyspondyly was evident.

nodes.description: Recurrent patellar dislocation is a persistent orthopedic complication.

evidence.reference_title: Longterm follow-up in chondrodysplasia punctata, tibia-metacarpal type, demonstrating natural history.
evidence.snippet: All patients had recurrent patella dislocation.

nodes.description: Short metacarpals, especially central metacarpals, are a defining hand feature.

evidence.snippet: Radiologic findings in the older child include shortness of tibiae and the 3rd and 4th metacarpals.

nodes.description: Disproportionate short stature can persist into adulthood.

evidence.reference_title: Longterm follow-up in chondrodysplasia punctata, tibia-metacarpal type, demonstrating natural history.
evidence.snippet: The patients attained adult heights of 152, 138, and 148 cm.

nodes.description: Short tibiae are part of the named pattern and can be marked on prenatal imaging.

evidence.reference_title: Prenatal diagnosis of chondrodysplasia punctata tibia-metacarpal type using multidetector CT and three-dimensional reconstruction.
evidence.snippet: On 3-D CT, the tibiae were much shorter than the fibulae, the humeri were very short and bowed, and severe platyspondyly was evident.

nodes.description: Lumbar spinal stenosis can develop as a late orthopedic complication and may require surgical decompression in adulthood.

evidence.reference_title: Longterm follow-up in chondrodysplasia punctata, tibia-metacarpal type, demonstrating natural history.
evidence.snippet: One patient suffered persisting back pain related to spinal stenosis and required lumbar laminectomy at the age of 26 years.
